refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve an operational availability equal to or greater than the customer-defined target over the agreed evaluation period.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was mostly atomic, focusing on meeting or exceeding a customer-defined availability target. It was refined to explicitly state the measurement context (operational availability) and the evaluation period, ensuring clarity and solution neutrality without implying how availability is achieved.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the operational availability of the NGHE solution over the agreed evaluation period and compare it against the customer-defined target to verify compliance.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall achieve an operational availability equal to or greater than the customer-defined target over the agreed evaluation period.

candidate_dps: Design the system with redundant critical components to ensure failover capability
Implement continuous monitoring and predictive maintenance to minimize downtime
Establish operational procedures and safety plans to reduce human error and operational interruptions
Incorporate modular design to isolate faults and enable quick repairs or replacements
Use real-time availability tracking and reporting to meet customer-defined targets
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design the system with redundant critical components to ensure failover capability

potential_sources: Dependency between redundancy mechanisms and monitoring systems
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on redundancy to achieve availability independently. While monitoring and operational procedures support availability, they can be modularized separately to maintain independence.

assumptions_risks: Assumes redundancy can be implemented without excessive cost or complexity
Risk that operational policies may not be strictly followed, impacting availability
Assumes monitoring and maintenance systems are effective and timely
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including monitoring and operational procedures as supporting DPs to ensure comprehensive coverage of availability factors.

im_boundary.name: Safety Services
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for ensuring system safety, including failover and redundancy of critical components

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts when failover or safety events occur

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives system status and sends safety-related telemetry data

module_containment_scores.notes: Very broad module; contains many functions beyond safety, leading to high diffusion of failover logic.
Physical system module; some redundancy may be implemented here but failover logic likely spans multiple systems.
Focuses on remote control; failover of critical components is not its primary responsibility.
Most specific and cohesive module for failover and redundancy; minimal diffusion outside this boundary.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Safety Services module is the most appropriate boundary to contain the DP of designing redundant critical components for failover, as it is focused on safety and failover capabilities, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle module for physical redundancy implementation.
Interfaces with other modules may require careful coordination to avoid hidden coupling.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If containment risks persist, consider refining the DP to explicitly separate physical redundancy (Vehicle) from failover logic (Safety Services) to improve modular clarity.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP of designing the system with redundant critical components to ensure failover capability directly addresses the FR of achieving operational availability equal to or greater than the customer-defined target. Redundancy and failover are standard and effective approaches to meet high availability requirements, aligning well with the acceptance criteria of measured availability ≥ 0.97.

diffusion_risks: Failover logic and redundancy implementation may be spread across multiple modules such as NGHE Solution and Vehicle, leading to potential diffusion risk.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The Safety Services module has the highest containment score (9) and lowest diffusion score (2), indicating it is the most cohesive and appropriate boundary to contain the DP. However, there is a noted risk of dependency on the Vehicle module for physical redundancy implementation, which may require coordination to avoid hidden coupling.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the Safety Services module is feasible by monitoring system availability metrics and safety alerts generated by this module, aligned with the acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the Safety Services module is clear and focused, minimizing diffusion and supporting traceability from FR to IM.

recommended_revisions: Consider refining the DP to explicitly separate physical redundancy responsibilities (to be implemented in the Vehicle module) from failover logic (contained within Safety Services) to improve modular clarity and reduce diffusion risk.
Establish clear interface contracts and coordination mechanisms between Safety Services and Vehicle modules to manage dependencies and avoid hidden coupling.
If diffusion risks persist, consider decomposing the NGHE Solution module to better isolate failover and redundancy functions within Safety Services.

decision.justification: Although all gates have passed, there are identified diffusion risks due to the spread of failover logic and redundancy implementation across multiple modules, which could lead to hidden coupling and reduced modular clarity. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to revise the current level to explicitly separate physical redundancy responsibilities (to be implemented in the Vehicle module) from failover logic (contained within Safety Services) and establish clear interface contracts to mitigate these risks before proceeding to further decomposition.

revise.why: To explicitly separate physical redundancy responsibilities from failover logic to improve modular clarity and reduce diffusion risk, and to establish clear interface contracts between Safety Services and Vehicle modules.

assumptions: The current DP adequately addresses the FR but has diffusion risks due to module boundary overlaps.
Safety Services module is the most cohesive boundary for failover logic containment.
Vehicle module is responsible for physical redundancy implementation.
open_risks: Diffusion of failover logic and redundancy implementation across multiple modules leading to hidden coupling.
Dependency on Vehicle module for physical redundancy may cause coordination challenges.
evidence_needed: Verification of modular boundaries and interface contracts between Safety Services and Vehicle modules.
Monitoring data confirming containment of failover logic within Safety Services module.
Assessment of diffusion risk reduction after DP refinement.
